founder_voice_quotes: Reproducibility is a deliberate design choice.
We are building a commons. Commons don't work that way.
If the Romulator architecture, the cooperative economics, or the #2260 Pledge framework only works when filtered through public relations, it does not work.
You build the Features — We're building the Board.
Help each other help ourselves. For the Keep.

title: Three-Week Synopsis — The Opening Gambit
founder_voice_quotes: I am giving away 80% of that IP portfolio — including 222 Crown Jewels with no prior art — into a defensive patent commons that makes the entire AI industry safer and approximately  billion a year cheaper to run
Zero enshittification surface: Cost+20% constitutionally locked.

A solo veteran with a small AI team built a production platform, filed 13 patents, and is now giving away most of it — because the only way to out-compete extraction is to make cooperation cheaper than defection.
The Romulator is the first proof. The Six Steps is the roadmap. The next three weeks are the landing.
That's just, like, six things.
Help each other help ourselves. For the Keep.

title: The Romulator 9000 — A Cooperative Patent Commons in Practice
founder_voice_quotes: You would look at that worker and say: this is insane. Strap the paint can to the painter's back.
The paint can is a million tokens large. The painter still walks.

Most AI systems today are built upside down. The LLM is treated as the coordinator, the specialist, the scheduler, the deduper, the orchestrator, the proofreader, and the audit log. The LLM is a specialist. Using it as the engine is like hiring a neurosurgeon to boil water.
The Romulator is the plumbing. Cheap. Reliable. Invisible.
We call this the Living Laboratory thesis: the thing building itself proves the architecture works.
The only way to out-compete extraction is to make cooperation cheaper than defection.
We are not reinventing AI. We are changing whose pocket the savings go into.
Help each other help ourselves. For the Keep.
